input.title: Freelancing Platform
input.description: Freelancing Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Freelancer Registration
requirements: Register with email and create profile
Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio

FR-03.title: Job Posting
requirements: Post jobs with description, budget, and timeline
Set required skills and experience level
Choose fixed-price or hourly contract type

FR-04.title: Job Search and Proposals
requirements: Search jobs by keyword, skill, and budget
Filter by job type, experience level, and duration
Submit proposals with cover letter and bid

FR-06.title: Escrow Payment System
requirements: Client funds escrow before work starts
Automatic weekly billing for hourly contracts

FR-07.title: Messaging and File Sharing
requirements: Real-time messaging between client and freelancer

FR-09.title: Freelancer Portfolio
requirements: Showcase completed projects with images and descriptions

FR-01.title: Plan Freelancer Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for freelancer registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Register with email and create profile; Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio; Identity verification process.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for freelancer registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for freelancer registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for freelancer registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Freelancer Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for freelancer registration on Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Register with email and create profile; Add skills, hourly rate, and portfolio; Identity verification process.
acceptance_criteria: Core freelancer registration business rules are implemented for Freelancing Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for freelancer registration
API/service behavior for freelancer registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Freelancer Registration Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for freelancer registration with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The freelancer registration user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for freelancer registration behave correctly
The freelancer registration UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Freelancer Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by freelancer registration, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by freelancer registration are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for freelancer registration integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for freelancer registration are handled securely

FR-01.title: Write Freelancer Registration Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for freelancer registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for freelancer registration are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for freelancer registration are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical freelancer registration scenarios

Indexes, constraints, and relationships for client registration are defined correctly
Migrations for client registration can be applied safely in target environments

FR-02.description: Implement the external integration points used by client registration, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. FR-02.title: Harden Client Registration Security and Access Rules
FR-02.description: Apply security controls to client registration. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for client registration are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in client registration are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for client registration passes review and automated checks

FR-02.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for client registration. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.

Regression coverage exists for the most critical client registration scenarios

FR-10.title: Plan Admin Platform Management Workflow and Contracts
FR-10.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for admin platform management and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: User verification and approval; Fee structure management; Dispute arbitration tools.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for admin platform management is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for admin platform management are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for admin platform management can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-10.title: Implement Admin Platform Management Backend Services
FR-10.description: Build the backend services for admin platform management on Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Stripe. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: User verification and approval; Fee structure management; Dispute arbitration tools.
acceptance_criteria: Core admin platform management business rules are implemented for Freelancing Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for admin platform management
API/service behavior for admin platform management is testable and documented for integration

FR-10.title: Implement Admin Platform Management Frontend Flow
FR-10.description: Build the frontend flow for admin platform management with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The admin platform management user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for admin platform management behave correctly
The admin platform management UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-10.title: Design Admin Platform Management Persistence Model
FR-10.description: Design the persistence layer for admin platform management. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for admin platform management supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for admin platform management are defined correctly
Migrations for admin platform management can be applied safely in target environments

FR-10.title: Implement Admin Platform Management Notifications and Workflow Triggers
FR-10.description: Add notifications and event-driven workflow triggers for admin platform management. Define when alerts should fire, how recipients are selected, which delivery channels are supported, and how notification failures are retried or surfaced to operations teams.
acceptance_criteria: Notification triggers for admin platform management fire on the correct workflow events
Recipients, templates, and delivery channels for admin platform management are configurable and testable
Notification failures for admin platform management are observable and recoverable

FR-10.title: Write Admin Platform Management Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for admin platform management. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for admin platform management are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for admin platform management are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical admin platform management scenarios
